entity.summary: Long-running centralized cryptocurrency exchange that was shut down after U.S. law-enforcement action in July 2017, including charges against alleged operator Alexander Vinnik and a FinCEN civil money penalty.
entity.notes: Death date follows the July 2017 law-enforcement action and practical shutdown of the BTC-e service. WEX is treated as related follow-up history rather than a successor_id here because succession and asset continuity were disputed.

events.title: BTC-e launched
events.description: BTC-e operated as a major fiat-to-crypto and crypto-to-crypto exchange beginning in 2011, especially serving Russian-language markets.
events.notes: Month-level launch date based on public background references.

events.title: BTC-e shut down after U.S. law-enforcement action
events.description: BTC-e went offline after U.S. law-enforcement action tied to charges against Alexander Vinnik and BTC-e for alleged money laundering and operation of an unlicensed money service business.
events.notes: Used as death_date because the exchange service stopped operating normally after this enforcement action.

events.title: FinCEN assessed civil money penalty
events.description: FinCEN assessed a 110 million USD civil money penalty against BTC-e for willful AML violations and a separate penalty against Alexander Vinnik.
events.notes: Follow-up regulatory action supporting the regulation death_reason.

evidence.title: BTC-e background reference
evidence.notes: Used only for basic launch-year and entity-background cross-checking.

evidence.title: Russian National And Bitcoin Exchange Charged In 21-Count Indictment
evidence.notes: Primary U.S. law-enforcement source for the indictment and BTC-e enforcement action.

evidence.title: FinCEN Fines BTC-e Virtual Currency Exchange $110 Million
evidence.notes: Primary regulatory source for the AML penalty and BTC-e enforcement context.

evidence.title: Who is Alexander Vinnik, the Russian prisoner being traded for American Marc Fogel?
evidence.notes: Later Reuters background source for BTC-e, Vinnik, money-laundering context, and the platform's scale.
